name: Licorería Limantour
address: Av. Álvaro Obregón 106, Roma Norte, 06700 Mexico City
description: The bar that launched Mexico City's modern cocktail era in April 2011 received the Rémy Martin Legend of the List Award in 2025—recognition of a venue that has appeared on every major ranking for over a decade. Bar Manager José Luis León Martínez, a 2010 World Class Mexico winner, runs the programme from the original Roma Norte location with a menu that rotates seasonally and celebrates Mexican roots. The Margarita al Pastor—tequila, orange liqueur, and a house-made taco-spice blend—remains the signature. At No. 9 in North America and No. 52 globally, Limantour is the foundation the rest of the scene stands on.

recommended_action: compare with active /satan-s-whiskers and delete loser
name: Satan's Whiskers
description: Named Best Bar in the UK by the Morning Advertiser and ranked No. 21 globally, this Bethnal Green stalwart doubled down in 2025 on precisely the thing that built its reputation: classic cocktails, daily-changing menus, and music loud enough to matter. Bar Manager Oliver Sagerström Blom leads a team that treats hospitality as performance art, turning a narrow East London room into the most energetic drinking experience in the city. Co-founders Damian Benjamin and Fraser Chapman have resisted every temptation to expand or franchise. The discipline shows in every glass.

recommended_action: compare with active /tlec-n and delete loser
name: Tlecān
description: Bernardo Serna and Eli Martínez Bello—who won the 2025 Altos Bartenders' Bartender Award—built a standing-room-only mezcalería on Álvaro Obregón whose name translates from Nahuatl as "place of fire." No reservations, no seats, and a programme that channels pre-Hispanic drinking traditions through thirteen mezcals sourced from small-batch makers, with emphasis on female distillers. The Paloma Blanca—carbonated clarified grapefruit, mezcal, lime, grapefruit salt—distils the philosophy into a single glass. At No. 3 in North America and No. 20 globally, Tlecān burns with conviction.

recommended_action: compare with active /martiny-s and delete loser
name: Martiny's
description: Takuma Watanabe spent eight years at Angel's Share before opening this three-floor carriage house on East 17th Street, where Japanese technique meets old New York architecture. Hand-carved ice, hot towels, and a Grand Martini built with gin, sherry, port, and cognac earned Watanabe the Bartender of the Year title at the 2024 Spirits Business Awards and the Best Bartender in North America nod at the Shaker Awards. Ranked No. 15 on North America's list, Martiny's treats every drink as a ceremony. The formality is the point.

recommended_action: compare with active /virt and delete loser
name: Virtù
address: 39th Floor, Four Seasons Hotel, Otemachi, Tokyo
description: Head Bartender Keith Motsi runs the Four Seasons Tokyo's cocktail programme at No. 45 globally, combining luxury hotel resources with a personal style shaped by years working across continents. The room delivers the polished service architecture that Four Seasons guests expect, but the drinks carry an edge—unexpected infusions, asymmetric flavour profiles, a willingness to challenge rather than merely comfort. Virtù won Tatler's bar award and sits at No. 18 on Asia's 50 Best. The hotel bar elevated into something more personal than institutional.
